description:

2. Pass affiliateAddress in X-Affiliate-Address header
3. Display affiliate fee in UI (already done, verify correctness)
4. Handle affiliate BPS from API response

Test: Widget with affiliateAddress prop, verify BPS flows correctly
title: Update widget to pass affiliate info correctly

description:
Modify packages/public-api to:

1. On X-Affiliate-Address header:
   - Lookup affiliate in microservices
   - Get their configured BPS (fallback to default if not found)
2. Pass affiliate BPS to swapper getTradeQuote/getTradeRate
3. Return affiliateBps and affiliateAddress in responses
4. Handle unknown affiliates gracefully (use default BPS)

Test: curl with X-Affiliate-Address header, verify correct BPS in response
title: Update public-api to lookup affiliate BPS

description:
Full QA testing with QABot:

1. Register test affiliate via dashboard
2. Set custom BPS (e.g., 100 = 1%)
3. Execute swap through widget with affiliate address
4. Verify:
   - Correct BPS applied to quote
   - Swap tracked with affiliate attribution
   - Dashboard shows swap in history
   - Revenue attribution correct

Create QABot fixture and push report to dashboard
title: QA test affiliate system end-to-end

description:
## Problem
Widget/public-api affiliates get hardcoded 10 BPS while web app uses 60 BPS. No way to:
- Store affiliate BPS per affiliate
- Track swaps by affiliate address
- Allow affiliates to configure their own BPS
- Use partner codes (vultisig, shapeshift, venice)

## Current State Investigation

### Web App (src/lib/fees/)
- `DEFAULT_FEE_BPS = '60'` hardcoded
- `getAffiliateBps()` returns 0 for related asset swaps, 60 otherwise
- No affiliate address/BPS storage

### Public API (packages/public-api/)
- Accepts `X-Affiliate-Address` header
- Returns `affiliateBps` in responses but doesn't store/configure it
- No affiliate BPS customization

### Widget (packages/swap-widget/)
- Accepts `affiliateAddress` prop
- Passes to API but BPS is server-side hardcoded

### Microservices (swap-service)
- Stores swaps with `referralCode` from user-service
- No affiliate address/BPS tracking
- No affiliate revenue attribution

## Proposed Solution Architecture

### Option A: Affiliate Dashboard + Microservices Storage
1. Create affiliate-dashboard app (Reown wallet auth on Arbitrum)
2. Affiliate registers wallet → claims partner code
3. Store in microservices: {walletAddress, partnerCode, bps, createdAt}
4. Widget/API lookup affiliate BPS by address before swap
5. Track all swaps by affiliate address

### Option B: Simpler On-Chain Approach
1. Use affiliate address as identifier
2. Store BPS config in microservices (no dashboard initially)
3. Partner codes map to affiliate addresses in config
4. Manual onboarding for new affiliates

### Option C: Smart Contract Registry
1. Deploy affiliate registry contract on Arbitrum
2. Affiliates register with wallet signature
3. On-chain BPS lookup for transparency

## Tasks to Break Down
1. Document current affiliate flow end-to-end
2. Design affiliate data model (address, partnerCode, bps, swaps)
3. Update microservices to store affiliate config
4. Update public-api to lookup affiliate BPS
5. Update widget to pass affiliate info
6. Create affiliate dashboard (if Option A)
7. Track swaps by affiliate for revenue attribution
8. QA test full flow
9. Update documentation
title: Affiliate System Alignment Spike

description:
Add partner code functionality:

1. Partner codes are short identifiers (vultisig, shapeshift, venice)
2. Map partner codes to affiliate addresses in DB
3. Widget/API can accept either:
   - X-Affiliate-Address header (direct wallet)
   - X-Partner-Code header (maps to affiliate)
4. One partner code can have multiple affiliate wallets (org support)
5. Partner code lookup resolves to affiliate config (BPS, etc.)

Example: vultisig partner code → their affiliate wallet → their BPS
title: Implement partner code system

description:
Add affiliate management to shapeshift/microservices:

1. Create Prisma model for affiliates
2. Add affiliate-service or extend swap-service:
   - GET /affiliates/:address - lookup affiliate by wallet
   - POST /affiliates - register new affiliate (with auth)
   - PATCH /affiliates/:address - update BPS/partnerCode
3. Add affiliate lookup in swap creation flow
4. Track affiliateAddress on swaps for revenue attribution

Test: Docker compose up and verify CRUD operations
title: Update microservices to store affiliate config

description:
Design the data model for affiliate management:

### Affiliate Table (microservices)
- id: UUID
- walletAddress: string (EVM address, primary identifier)
- partnerCode: string (optional, e.g., 'vultisig', 'venice')
- bps: number (basis points, e.g., 60)
- isActive: boolean
- createdAt, updatedAt timestamps

### Affiliate Swap Tracking
- Extend swap table with affiliateId/affiliateAddress
- Or create separate affiliate_swaps junction table

### Partner Codes
- Map partner codes to affiliate wallets
- Allow multiple wallets per partner code (organization)

Output: Prisma schema additions for microservices
title: Design affiliate data model

description:
Update docs for affiliate system:

1. Public API docs: X-Affiliate-Address, X-Partner-Code headers
2. Widget README: affiliateAddress prop usage
3. Affiliate onboarding guide: how to register, claim partner code
4. Revenue attribution: how fees work, payout process

Locations:
- packages/public-api/README.md
- packages/swap-widget/README.md
- docs/affiliates.md (new)
title: Update affiliate documentation

description:
Add affiliate revenue tracking:

1. On swap completion, record:
   - affiliateAddress
   - swapId
   - sellAmount/buyAmount USD values
   - affiliate fee earned (BPS * sellAmount)
2. Aggregate queries for affiliate dashboard:
   - Total swaps by affiliate
   - Total volume by affiliate
   - Total fees earned by affiliate
3. Time-series data for charts

This enables: 'Show me all swaps done through my affiliate address'
title: Track swaps by affiliate for revenue attribution

description:
Extend existing packages/affiliate-dashboard:

**Current state:**
- Basic stats display (swaps, volume, fees)
- Address input (no auth)
- Period filtering
- Fetches from /v1/affiliate/stats

**Add:**
1. Reown (WalletConnect) wallet auth on Arbitrum
2. Sign message to prove wallet ownership
3. After auth:
   - View/update configured BPS
   - Claim partner code (if available)
   - View full swap history through affiliate address
   - View revenue attribution breakdown
4. Connect to microservices affiliate endpoints (when created)

**Stack additions:**
- @reown/appkit or wagmi + viem
- Arbitrum chain config
- SIWE (Sign-In with Ethereum) for auth
title: Update affiliate-dashboard with wallet auth and BPS config

description:
Map out the complete affiliate data flow:
- Web app: getAffiliateBps() → swapperApi → swapper packages
- Widget: affiliateAddress prop → API calls
- Public API: X-Affiliate-Address header handling
- Microservices: swap-service affiliate tracking (or lack thereof)

Create a mermaid diagram showing data flow and identify gaps.
title: Document current affiliate flow end-to-end
